notes: Both configurations pass 5/5 assertions on all three evals (delta +0.00). The assertions do not discriminate because the baseline (Claude Code running inside this repo) also reads clearcontract_project_plan.md unprompted — the plan being the only file in the repo makes grounding nearly automatic.

The skill's observable value in this iteration is behavioral, not pass/fail: all 3 with-skill runs checked git state and anchored advice to 'nothing built yet'; at most 1 of 3 baselines did. All 3 with-skill runs closed by offering to record the decision in docs/decisions.md for future sessions; no baseline did. With-skill runs added re-litigation guards ('this is the last free re-litigation', 'the bar is evidence, not vibes').

Expect the skill's delta to grow as the repo fills with code: the baseline found the plan easily today because it is essentially the only file; once source code dominates the tree, unprompted plan-grounding is much less likely, while the skill mandates it plus a git-state check.
Cost of the skill is modest: with-skill runs averaged ~40.6k tokens vs ~33.7k baseline (+~20%), mostly from the mandated plan read + git check.

Watch-item for iteration 2: on pocketbase-vs-supabase, both configurations recommended switching stacks. If the user considers that wrong (the skill says re-litigating the stack 'needs strong evidence, not vibes'), the skill needs a stronger status-quo prior for settled decisions.
